This is a **Next.js 15 App Router** project using **React 19**, **TypeScript**, and **Tailwind CSS v4**. Authentication is handled via **next-auth v5** with bcrypt password hashing. Data layer is **Supabase** (Postgres) with SSR support, plus **better-sqlite3** for local DB operations. The app has public pages (login, register, landing) and a dashboard with portfolio, strategies, settings, watchlist, and predictions features.

│       │   └── StrategiesClient.tsx   # "use client" — 3 AI strategies with toggle switches (Trend, Reversion, Sentiment)
│       └── settings/
│           ├── page.tsx               # Server component — thin wrapper, renders SettingsClient
│           └── SettingsClient.tsx     # "use client" — email change form, profile info, danger zone
│
├── frontend/
│   └── lib/
│       ├── types.ts                   # User, WatchlistItem, Prediction interfaces
│       ├── db.ts                      # JSON file-based DB (users.json, watchlists.json, predictions.json)
│       ├── auth.ts                    # Supabase SSR auth helper (with cookie fallback)
│       ├── supabase.ts                # Dynamic client factory (fallback if @supabase/supabase-js missing)
│       ├── predictions.ts             # Seeded deterministic prediction engine per ticker
│       └── api/

│           └── watchlist.ts           # GET/POST/DELETE /api/watchlist — CRUD with auth

## package.json — Tech Stack

| Category | Technology | Version |
|---|---|---|
| **Framework** | Next.js (App Router, Turbopack) | ^15.3.3 |
| **UI** | React + React DOM | ^19.1.0 |
| **Language** | TypeScript | ^5.8.3 |
| **CSS** | Tailwind CSS v4 + `@tailwindcss/postcss` | ^4.1.0 |
| **Auth** | next-auth | ^5.0.0-beta.25 |
| **Password hashing** | bcryptjs | ^2.4.3 |
| **Database (cloud)** | @supabase/supabase-js + @supabase/ssr | ^2.50.0 / ^0.6.1 |
| **Database (local)** | better-sqlite3 | ^12.10.1 |

---

## What This Repo Is

**Goon** — an autonomous AI trading platform for retail investors. It's a Next.js 15 app with:

1. **Public landing page** — "Autonomous AI Trading Agents" hero with 3 feature cards
2. **Auth flow** — Supabase email/password login & registration (client-side forms)
3. **Protected dashboard** — middleware redirects unauthenticated users away from `/dashboard`
4. **Dashboard sections**: Overview (welcome + portfolio cards + activity table), Portfolio (holdings table), Strategies (3 AI strategies with toggle enable/disable), Settings (profile + email change + danger zone)
5. **Dual data layer**: Supabase (Postgres) for auth/production, JSON file-based storage (`frontend/lib/db.ts`) for local/dev
6. **API routes**: `/api/predictions` (seeded AI predictions per watchlist ticker) and `/api/watchlist` (full CRUD)
7. **Custom theme**: Tailwind v4 `@theme` with `navy`, `cobalt`, `accent` (green), `navy-100` colors

The app has mock/hardcoded data in the dashboard and portfolio pages (static arrays), plus functional API endpoints backed by the JSON file DB.

## 1) PRODUCT
Goon is a full-stack automated marketing SaaS for mid-sized real estate brokerages (20–200 agents, $20M–$100M revenue). It delivers AI-driven, hyper-local, closed-loop campaigns (SEO, PPC, social, email, content) with service-configurable packages, booking/demo scheduling, lead capture forms, campaign dashboards, and recurring subscription billing. The core value is turning realtor marketing intent into booked demos and closed leads via automated, localized campaigns — solving the pain of fragmented tools, manual workflows, and opaque performance (research: ICP mid-sized brokerages, $2k–$10k/mo SaaS + 5–15% performance fee). The primary user is real-estate marketing ops/manager and broker owners who need an all-in-one control center with real metrics and simple package configuration.

## 2) WHO IT’S FOR
ICP: mid-sized real estate brokerages (20–200 agents, $20M–$100M revenue) in AZ/CA. Personas: Marketing Manager (time-poor, needs set-and-forget automation), Broker/Owner (wants ROI and lead volume), Exec Assistant (handles scheduling/payments). ## 3) LOOK & FEEL
- Vibe: warm SaaS with rounded surfaces, soft shadows, generous white space, prism-inspired subtle gradients.

- Typography: Manrope (headings), Source Sans 3 (body), Inter (ui elements), JetBrains Mono (pricing/metrics mono accents).
- Layout: single-column top-down, consistent max-w-6xl container, 24–32px section vertical rhythm, 8px base unit.
- Components: rounded buttons (12px), raised cards (8–16px radius), subtle shadow-sm, border focus rings in violet, iconography outlined style with coral highlights.
- Motion: ease-out cubic, 200–300ms micro-interactions, staggered list fade-in.

Main screens:
- Landing (kept intact): Hero (value prop + primary CTA), Features (3), Pricing (3 tiers), FAQ, Footer.
- Auth: centered card with violet header, email+password + magic-link fallback, minimal chrome.
- Dashboard (Today): top bar (brand + global nav), Today card (campaign KPIs, lead metrics, quick actions), Secondary nav (Campaigns, Bookings, Forms, Subscriptions, Admin).
- Campaign Detail: header with status badges, tabs (Overview, Leads, Automations, Settings).

## 6) CORE FEATURES
- Auth: NextAuth v5 Credentials provider with bcrypt, session via JWT, email verification, magic link.
- Customer Portal/Dashboard: Today view with KPIs (leads, booked demos, revenue), campaign list, subscription status, quick CTAs.

- Booking/Demo Scheduler: Calendly-style availability picker integrated with Calendly API, timezone handling, buffer times, confirmation and email invite via Resend.
- Admin Dashboard: customer CRUD, campaign CRUD, role management, audit logs.
- Contact/Lead Capture Forms: drag-and-drop form builder, field validation, webhook to CRM/email, submissions list.
- Stripe Integration: subscription creation, checkout session, webhook handler for events (invoice.paid, customer.subscription.updated), proration, cancel_at_period_end.
- Email Notifications: Resend integration (send verification, booking confirm, campaign alerts, invoice receipts), templates stored in /emails.
- PostHog Analytics: middleware event capture (signup, login, package_select, checkout_start, subscription, booking, lead_capture), session replay optional.

## 7) DATA MODEL
- User { id, email, name, password_hash, email_verified, role (customer|admin), created_at, stripe_customer_id }
- Campaign { id, user_id, name, package_id, status (draft|active|paused), geo, keywords, ad_spend, cpc, start_date, end_date, created_at, updated_at }
- CampaignMetric { id, campaign_id, date, impressions, clicks, leads, spend, conversions }

- Booking { id, user_id, customer_id, package_id, slot_start, slot_end, status (scheduled|confirmed|cancelled), metadata }
- Subscription { id, user_id, stripe_subscription_id, package_id, status (active|trialing|canceled), current_period_start, current_period_end, cancel_at_period_end }
- Form { id, user_id, name, fields JSON, submit_url, created_at }

- EmailTemplate { id, name, subject, html, variables JSON }

## 10) ACCEPTANCE
- Auth: sign up with email/password creates verified user; magic link works; protected routes redirect unauthenticated users.
- Dashboard: Today view shows KPIs and last 5 campaigns; data loads via server actions.

For the landing page, I'll preserve the existing design while updating the branding from trading to marketing.

I'm organizing this into batches to write efficiently: first the core setup files like the database schema and auth config, then the UI components, then the layout files, and finally all the page routes. This way I can build up the architecture systematically and ensure everything connects properly. I'm setting up the complete data model with users, campaigns, leads, bookings, subscriptions, forms, and templates. For the in-memory store, I'll create a singleton that loads from a JSON file on disk, seeds demo data if it doesn't exist, and provides async read/write methods to handle concurrent requests safely.

# Goon — Automated Marketing for Realtors

AI-driven, hyper-local, closed-loop marketing campaigns for real estate brokerages. SEO, PPC, social, email, content — all automated.

## Tech Stack

- **Framework**: Next.js 15 (App Router, Turbopack)
- **UI**: React 19, Tailwind CSS v4
- **Auth**: NextAuth v5 Credentials (email/password, bcrypt)
- **Database**: JSON file-based (dev) / Prisma + PostgreSQL (prod)
- **Payments**: Stripe (subscriptions, checkout)
- **Email**: Resend
- **Analytics**: PostHog
- **Language**: TypeScript

## Quick Start

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Visit `http://localhost:3000` and click "Try Live Demo" to log in with the demo account.

## Demo Account

- **Email**: demo@demo.app
- **Password**: demo123
- **Role**: Customer (full dashboard access)

## Admin Account

- **Email**: admin@goon.app
- **Password**: admin123
- **Role**: Admin (customer + campaign management)

## Features

- Landing page with warm SaaS design system
- Email/password authentication via NextAuth v5 + bcrypt
- Customer dashboard with KPIs, campaigns, bookings
- Service configurator (5 packages: SEO, PPC, Social, Email, Content)
- Calendly-style booking scheduler
- Lead capture form builder
- Stripe subscription management
- Admin dashboard for customer/campaign management
- Mobile responsive layout
- PostHog analytics integration
- Resend email notifications

## Project Structure

```
app/
├── auth/           # Sign in, sign up, verify
├── dashboard/      # Customer portal
├── admin/          # Admin panel
├── api/            # API routes
components/
├── ui/             # Reusable UI (Button, Card, Input, DatePicker)
├── dashboard/      # Dashboard-specific (Sidebar, StatsCard, CampaignCard, etc.)
├── contact/        # Contact form
├── campaign-kpi.tsx
├── service-configurator.tsx
├── booking-calendar.tsx
lib/
├── auth.ts         # NextAuth config + session helpers
├── db.ts           # JSON file-based database + seed data
├── stripe.ts       # Stripe integration
├── email.ts        # Resend email templates
├── analytics.ts    # PostHog event capture
├── validation.ts   # Zod schemas
```

## Environment Variables

Copy `.env.example` to `.env.local` and fill in your keys. The app works in demo mode without any external service keys.

// This is your Prisma schema file
// Learn more: https://pris.ly/d/prisma-schema

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

model User {
  id               String         @id @default(uuid())
  email            String         @unique
  name             String
  passwordHash     String         @map("password_hash")
  emailVerified    Boolean        @default(false) @map("email_verified")
  role             String         @default("customer")
  stripeCustomerId String?        @map("stripe_customer_id")
  createdAt        DateTime       @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt        DateTime       @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
  campaigns        Campaign[]
  bookings         Booking[]
  subscriptions    Subscription[]
  forms            MarketingForm[]

  @@map("users")
}

model Campaign {
  id        String           @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String           @map("user_id")
  name      String
  packageId String           @map("package_id")
  status    String           @default("draft")
  geo       String
  keywords  String[]
  adSpend   Float            @default(0) @map("ad_spend")
  cpc       Float            @default(0)
  startDate DateTime         @map("start_date")
  endDate   DateTime?        @map("end_date")
  createdAt DateTime         @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt DateTime         @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
  user      User             @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
  metrics   CampaignMetric[]
  leads     Lead[]

  @@map("campaigns")
}

model CampaignMetric {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  campaignId  String   @map("campaign_id")
  date        DateTime
  impressions Int      @default(0)
  clicks      Int      @default(0)
  leads       Int      @default(0)
  spend       Float    @default(0)
  conversions Int      @default(0)
  campaign    Campaign @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id])

  @@map("campaign_metrics")
}

model Lead {
  id          String    @id @default(uuid())
  campaignId  String?   @map("campaign_id")
  source      String
  status      String    @default("new")
  name        String
  email       String
  phone       String?
  notes       String?
  capturedAt  DateTime  @map("captured_at")
  campaign    Campaign? @relation(fields: [campaignId], references: [id])

  @@map("leads")
}

model Booking {
  id          String    @id @default(uuid())
  userId      String    @map("user_id")
  customerId  String?   @map("customer_id")
  packageId   String?   @map("package_id")
  slotStart   DateTime  @map("slot_start")
  slotEnd     DateTime  @map("slot_end")
  status      String    @default("scheduled")
  metadata    String?
  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  user        User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])

  @@map("bookings")
}

model Subscription {
  id                     String    @id @default(uuid())
  userId                 String    @map("user_id")
  stripeSubscriptionId   String    @map("stripe_subscription_id")
  packageId              String    @map("package_id")
  status                 String    @default("active")
  currentPeriodStart     DateTime  @map("current_period_start")
  currentPeriodEnd       DateTime  @map("current_period_end")
  cancelAtPeriodEnd      Boolean   @default(false) @map("cancel_at_period_end")
  createdAt              DateTime  @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  user                   User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])

  @@map("subscriptions")
}

model MarketingForm {
  id        String          @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String          @map("user_id")
  name      String
  fields    Json
  submitUrl String          @map("submit_url")
  createdAt DateTime        @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  user      User            @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
  submissions FormSubmission[]

  @@map("marketing_forms")
}

model FormSubmission {
  id        String         @id @default(uuid())
  formId    String         @map("form_id")
  data      Json
  createdAt DateTime       @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  form      MarketingForm  @relation(fields: [formId], references: [id])

  @@map("form_submissions")
}
